title: Weekly Vessel Valuations Report, February 17 2026
summary:
Main
Bulkers: VV Bulker values remain mostly stable this week with gains observed for older Capesizes.Continued buyer interest across segments supports a balanced S&P market
Capesize Epic (182,100 DWT, Jul 2010, Odense Steel) sold by Nereus Shipping for USD 32.3 mil, VV Value USD 32.12 mil
Capesize Smyrna (176,900 DWT, Feb 2008, Namura) sold DD Due to Unknown Chinese buyers for USD 25.25 mil, VV Value USD 24.96 mil
Kamsarmax Anglo Red & Anglo Barinthus (81,600-81,700 DWT, 2013, Jiangsu Guoxin) sold by Anglo International Shipping for USD 34 mil en bloc, VV Value USD 31.13 mil
Handysize Sigma Venture (34,700 DWT, Jun 2012, Chengxi Shipyard) sold DD Passed by Sigma Shipping USD 11.75 mil, VV Value USD 12.21 mil
Handysize African Lark & African Dove (34,400 DWT, May 2014, Namura) sold by MUR Shipping for USD 33 mil en bloc, VV Value USD 33.02 mil
Tankers: VV Tanker values firmed across the board again this week with mid age and older tonnage seeing the greatest gains. Secondhand activity was strong across all sectors and ages while three more VLCCs were added to Sinokor's growing fleet list
VLCC Ilma & Ingrid (314,000 DWT, 2012, Hyundai Heavy Ind Ulsan Shipyard) sold By CMB Tech to Si
